frames: The agent starts in a low crouch with all four legs on the ground, then lowers its body further.
Its body is nearly touching the ground, and its legs are widely splayed, maintaining ground contact.
The agent begins to push its body upwards, drawing its legs inward slightly.
It returns to a stable, crouched posture with all four legs firmly on the checkered surface.
The agent once again lowers its body, extending its legs outwards from its central mass.
Its body is very close to the ground, and it extends its front-right and rear-left legs further.
The agent maintains a very low posture, with its front-right leg extended far forward.
It then pushes its body up, bringing its legs back to a balanced, crouched stance.
